reasoning:
The question asks whether Aleksandra Goryachkina's classical Elo rating on the Wikipedia rank list will exceed her current 2541 by at least 1% (meaning her rating must hit >= 2566.41). For the November dates (Nov 2 and Nov 25), the data will come from the November 1 FIDE baseline. This list will include her games from the European Club Cup, which concluded in late October. In that tournament, she scored silver on Board 1 but crucially drew against much lower-rated opposition (like Ushenina, rated 2407), which mathematically prevents a 25.4 point gain over a 7-round event (such a gain would require beating almost entirely higher-rated opposition or scoring 7/7 against equal opposition). Thus, reaching 2566.41 by November is practically impossible. 

For January 2026, she has roughly two months to gain over 25 points. While she has previously achieved large gains when in top form (e.g., +17.7 at Shymkent Grand Prix in 2024), gaining 25.4 points in fewer than 20 games requires scoring extraordinarily well in elite tournaments, which is rare for an established 2550 player without a rapid string of upsets. Her peak was higher (2611), but given her recent stabilization around the mid-2500s, this spike is highly improbable in such a short window. I predict a very low chance (~2%) for the November dates, since FIDE operates on monthly lists and it's sealed, and a slightly higher but still low chance (~5%) for January, accounting for the remote possibility of an explosive performance in late 2025 classical events.
